title: Renaissance Hermeticism
subtitle: Florence, the Corpus Hermeticum, and the quintessence, 15th–16th centuries

narrative.intro: Ficino's translation of the Hermetic corpus reframes alchemy within a prisca theologia. Italy and the Low Countries dominate; alchemy fuses with natural philosophy and the search for a quintessence.
narrative.provenance_note: Events from the ALCHEMYTIMELINEMAP corpus (largely draft-status entries); locations are event geocodes, not residences. Transmission arcs are reconstructed from the dated itineraries of named figures — absence of an arc reflects gaps in the record, not certainty of no contact.
narrative.design_note: Copperplate Engraving: the era print culture took over; sepia line-work is the register of the humanist printed book.
